figures.caption: Figure 1: Empirical dynamics of PEIRA on CIFAR-10. PEIRA on a single training run. Left: alignment αi = e⊤i NU,V ei/(∥ei∥ ∥NU,V ei∥) ∈ [0, 1] of the top eigenvectors ei of the signal ΣU,V with respect to the noise NU,V during training. αi = 1 implies ei is also an eigenvector of NU,V . Both matrices gradually align their eigenvectors during training. Middle: spectrum of ΣU,V during training develops additional significant modes. Right: validation top-1 and the negative PEIRA objective −EPEIRA during training are strongly correlated.

Figure 2: Study of PEIRA regularization on ImageNet-1K. ResNet-50, 100 epochs, frozen PEIRA configuration of Table G.1; 1–3 random seeds per cell. Left: sensitivity of the final online linear-probe top-1 accuracy to the regularizer λ; each dot is one sweep cell, pooling over ηmin, optimizer weight decay, and gradient clipping. Middle: backbone entropy-based effective rank [Garrido et al., 2023a] versus epoch, colored by λ; smaller λ accumulates rank faster in the early phase. Right: online linear-probe top-1 versus the negative objective −EPEIRA (mirroring the rightmost CIFAR-10 panel of Figure 1), evaluated for checkpoints at epochs 25, 50, 75, 100 across runs spanning various λ values; each dot is one (cell, checkpoint) pair, pooling over ηmin, optimizer weight decay, and gradient clipping. Per-λ Spearman correlation ρ is reported in the right-panel legend.
